gaps: score.sh at 1200+ lines — too much in one file, needs decomposition
synthesize.sh --text mode has no color/bars — inconsistent with score.sh style
~20 remaining 2>/dev/null for legitimate ops (stat, find) — not critical but noisy
mktemp error capture pattern repeated 4x — not DRY
no automated tests for error communication paths
strengths: boot card passes 5-second test with contextual numbers: assertions show % + maturity impact
prediction accuracy shows calibration context (target: 50-70%)
stage ceiling shown inline when score exceeds it
synthesize.sh --text mode with per-feature direction hints and bottleneck identification
critical error swallows replaced with actionable messages (eval failure, cache corruption, missing paths)
eval-cache pre-validation deduplicates error output
formula documented in header (score.sh:7-45)
score trend creates return trigger
scoring.evidence: Product surface now teaches instead of just displaying. Numbers have context (85% — 9 failures block polished). Errors communicate recovery actions. synthesize.sh --text is a new formatted output mode. Critical 2>/dev/null sites fixed with actionable messages. score.sh size remains the top craft gap.

gaps: experiment-learnings.md is append-only — 55 patterns, no staleness detection
grade.sh at 1538 lines — largest file in codebase
no test suite for 23 grading patterns
model_update column often empty even on wrong predictions
session_start.sh handles grading display — should be in grade.sh
strengths: auto-grade on session boot — zero manual intervention
23 grading patterns in grade.sh
prediction accuracy shows calibration context: 60% accurate (target: 50-70%)
out-of-range accuracy gets diagnosis: >70% = too safe, <50% = model needs updating
prediction accuracy trend on boot card
trail.sh cleanly scoped at 258 lines
learning.evidence: Mechanical loop works and now communicates meaning. Accuracy context added (target: 50-70%). Still missing staleness detection and model_update enforcement.

gaps: 0 external users — all depth is self-validated
evidence-scan.sh fails in plugin mode (path resolution)
scripts assume repo root but plugin cache changes working dir
voice.md compliance unverified — no mechanical check
each skill has its own state-gathering script reading overlapping sources
no shared state layer across skills
strengths: 27 skills with consistent folder structure (SKILL.md + scripts/ + references/ + gotchas.md)
intent routing covers 20+ intents
/plan output passes 5-second test — bottleneck named, moves proposed
cross-skill synthesis suggests next command
14 named agents with model tiers
commands.evidence: Skills are deep and well-structured. Intent routing is genuine value. But untested on real users, plugin mode has known path resolution bugs, and voice.md compliance is aspirational not verified.

gaps: expected_skill_count hardcoded as 21 but actual is 27 (install.sh:319)
no uninstall path
plugin install is recommended but install.sh is irrelevant for that path
post-install doesn't suggest 'now run /rhino' — just shows check results
color helpers duplicated across 4+ files
strengths: opens with ◆ rhino-os install and dependency checks — clear 5-second test
missing jq shows specific install command per OS
--check dry-run, --test self-test
GNU/BSD fallbacks, idempotent design
install.evidence: Good user-facing design for manual install path. But plugin mode (recommended) bypasses install.sh entirely — first experience is session_start hook. Hardcoded skill count produces false warning.

gaps: walkthrough output stale — shows 68/56 but current is 88/51
no 'your first 5 minutes' quickstart section
no clear next action after reading README — what does the founder DO?
headline is just 'rhino-os' — METR hook is in paragraph 1, not the title
comparison table uses markdown table — voice.md says no tables
README violates its own product's formatting standard
strengths: UVP clear: product quality not code quality
METR -19% study hook is compelling positioning
real walkthrough with actual output (even if stale)
dual install instructions (plugin + manual)
docs.evidence: Strong UVP and positioning. Fails on user understanding: stale walkthrough data undermines credibility, no quickstart for the impatient, no clear first action. The front door doesn't practice what the product preaches.

gaps: smart promote doesn't actually read eval-cache for bottleneck features
grep/sed YAML parsing breaks on multiline values
no empty state guidance — what if there are no todos?
output doesn't suggest next action — it's a list, not a guide
todo.sh at 1177 lines for a backlog manager
commodity feature with undifferentiated UX
strengths: complete CRUD: health/add/list/done/promote/decay/import
file locking, YAML validation, duplicate ID detection
TTY-aware colors
todo.evidence: Robust backlog system but commodity. Promote/decay are novel but promote doesn't use eval data. Empty state unhandled. Output is data without direction.
